mapping.a_element: ramping rate of the relative phase between the periodic potentials (the adiabaticity-controlling drive rate)
mapping.b_element: rate of the time-dependent inflaton background (slow-rolling during inflation, oscillating during preheating)

mapping.a_element: near-adiabatic ramping that breaks perfect adiabatic following
mapping.b_element: non-adiabatic production events when the WKB/adiabatic approximation fails

mapping.a_element: finite Bogoliubov excitations (quasiparticles excited above the gap-soliton mean field)
mapping.b_element: produced right-handed Majorana-neutrino quanta / occupation numbers in the Hamiltonian-diagonal (instantaneous) basis

mapping.a_element: instantaneous mode structure set by soliton chemical potential vs. Bloch topological band
mapping.b_element: instantaneous Hamiltonian-diagonal basis with helicity-dependent effective momentum used to define occupation

mapping.a_element: spectral band gap of the soliton that sets the scale for non-adiabatic excitation
mapping.b_element: effective mass gap / momentum cutoff that sets when non-adiabatic production is efficient

mapping.a_element: particle loss / depletion of the solitons from the excited quasiparticles
mapping.b_element: resulting integrated neutrino abundance from the produced quanta

brief_justification: Both are governed by the same transferable machinery: a mode with a time-dependent effective spectrum driven non-adiabatically produces excitations, counted as Bogoliubov occupation in the instantaneous (Hamiltonian-diagonal) basis when the adiabatic theorem breaks down. In A the near-adiabatic ramp of the periodic-potential phase excites finite Bogoliubov quasiparticles that deplete the soliton; in B the rolling/oscillating inflaton drives non-adiabatic production of neutrino quanta whose occupation is defined in the Hamiltonian-diagonal basis, so the adiabatic-breakdown / Bogoliubov-transformation core is shared even though the topological (Chern-number) and leptogenesis superstructures differ.
